characters.name: Mr. Bennet
characters.description: Father of five daughters, sarcastic and detached, takes quiet amusement in his wife's nerves.

characters.name: Mrs. Bennet
characters.description: Mother obsessed with marrying off her daughters to wealthy men. Drives the action in Chapter 1.

characters.name: Jane Bennet
characters.description: Eldest Bennet daughter, beautiful and gentle, catches Bingley's attention at the Meryton assembly.

characters.name: Elizabeth Bennet
characters.description: Second eldest Bennet daughter, witty and perceptive, slighted by Darcy at the assembly.

characters.name: Mr. Bingley
characters.description: Newly arrived wealthy bachelor who takes Netherfield Park, amiable and handsome.

characters.name: Mr. Darcy
characters.description: Bingley's proud and wealthy friend, refuses to dance, considered disagreeable by the assembly.

characters.name: Charlotte Lucas
characters.description: Elizabeth's sensible friend, daughter of Sir William Lucas.

characters.name: Lydia Bennet
characters.description: Youngest Bennet daughter, forward and bold.

characters.name: Mary Bennet
characters.description: Middle Bennet daughter, studious and moralistic.

settings.name: Longbourn
settings.description: The Bennet family home in Hertfordshire.

settings.name: Meryton assembly hall
settings.description: Public assembly rooms where the neighbourhood gathers to dance.

settings.name: Netherfield Park
settings.description: Large estate newly let by Mr. Bingley.

plot_threads.thread: Bingley and Jane attraction — Bingley singles out Jane at the assembly, dances with her twice
Darcy's pride and Elizabeth's prejudice — Darcy slights Elizabeth, she forms a negative opinion of him
Mrs. Bennet's campaign to marry her daughters to wealthy men

world_rules: Marriage and social standing are intertwined; a single man in possession of a good fortune must be in want of a wife
The entail on Longbourn means the estate will pass away from the Bennet daughters
